question:
Why is "tone policing" — criticizing how someone expresses their grievance rather than addressing the grievance itself — considered a derailing tactic?
A) Emotional expression is governed by strict legal standards of acceptability.
B) It shifts the conversation from the validity of the complaint to the manner of its delivery, allowing the underlying issue to go unaddressed.
C) Tone policing always results in a more productive conversation.
D) It is only a derailing tactic when used in written communication.
